entries.definition:
جُنْد jund [coll. n.] host, army, helpers (67:20) أَمَّنْ هَذَا الَّذِي هُوَ جُنْدٌ لَكُمْ يَنْصُرُكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ who is this one who will be an army for you to defend you against the Beneficent?
جُنُود junūd [pl. of n. جُنْد jund] army, host, helpers, companions (27:17) وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَانَ جُنُودُهُ مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ وَالطَّيْرِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ and marshalled to Solomon were his hosts of jinn, humankind and birds and they were set in battle order.
entries.definition_english:
جُنْد jund [coll. n.] host, army, helpers (67:20) أَمَّنْ هَذَا الَّذِي هُوَ جُنْدٌ لَكُمْ يَنْصُرُكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ who is this one who will be an army for you to defend you against the Beneficent?
جُنُود junūd [pl. of n. جُنْد jund] army, host, helpers, companions (27:17) وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَانَ جُنُودُهُ مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ وَالطَّيْرِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ and marshalled to Solomon were his hosts of jinn, humankind and birds and they were set in battle order.

entries.definition:
j–n–n to shield, to cover, to engulf, shield, enclosure, place of hiding; the world of the jinn, the hidden people; night; grave; heart, inside, foetus; garden, verdant; madness; snake. Of this root, 10 forms occur 201 times in the Qurʾan: جَنّ jann once; جَانّ jānn seven times; جِنّ jinn 22 times; جِنَّة jinnatun 10 times; مَجْنُون majnūn 11 times; جَنَّة jannatun 70 times; جَنَّتَان jannatān eight times; جَنَّات jannāt 69 times; جُنَّة junnatun twice and أَجِنَّة ʾajinnatun once.
جَنَّ janna u [v. trans./intrans. with prep. عَلَى] to cover up, to hide, to descend (6:76) فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اللَّيْلُ رَأَى كَوْكَبًا when the night descended over him he saw a star.

جَانّ jānn [nominalised act. part. used collectively; pl. (or a quasi-pl. of) جِنّ jinn] (members of a parallel but hidden world of invisible people, beings, forces, spirits, angels or a kind of demon and the like, both good and bad (see app. III of Muhammad

176
entries.definition_english:
j–n–n to shield, to cover, to engulf, shield, enclosure, place of hiding; the world of the jinn, the hidden people; night; grave; heart, inside, foetus; garden, verdant; madness; snake. Of this root, 10 forms occur 201 times in the Qurʾan: جَنّ jann once; جَانّ jānn seven times; جِنّ jinn 22 times; جِنَّة jinnatun 10 times; مَجْنُون majnūn 11 times; جَنَّة jannatun 70 times; جَنَّتَان jannatān eight times; جَنَّات jannāt 69 times; جُنَّة junnatun twice and أَجِنَّة ʾajinnatun once.
جَنَّ janna u [v. trans./intrans. with prep. عَلَى] to cover up, to hide, to descend (6:76) فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اللَّيْلُ رَأَى كَوْكَبًا when the night descended over him he saw a star.

جَانّ jānn [nominalised act. part. used collectively; pl. (or a quasi-pl. of) جِنّ jinn] (members of a parallel but hidden world of invisible people, beings, forces, spirits, angels or a kind of demon and the like, both good and bad (see app. III of Muhammad

176

entries.definition:
Asad’s The Message of the Qurʾan)); specific usages are contextually determined: 1 (as opposed to humans) the jinn (55:14–15) خَلَقَ الْإِنْسَانَ مِنْ صَلْصَالٍ كَالْفَخَّارِ، وَخَلَقَ الْجَانَّ مِنْ مَارِجٍ مِنْ نَارٍ He created mankind out of dried clay, like pottery, the jinn out of a flame of smokeless fire 2 a big snake, serpent (in one interpretation of 27:10) وَأَلْقِ عَصَاكَ فَلَمَّا رَآهَا تَهْتَزُّ كَأَنَّهَا جَانٌّ وَلَّى مُدْبِرًا وَلَمْ يُعَقِّبْ ‘and throw down your staff’, but when he saw it writhing like a snake (or, a demon), he turned and fled, and did not come back.
جِنّ jinn [coll. n/pl. (or sing.) of جَانّ jānn] 1 the jinn (as opposed to humans) (51:56) وَمَا خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنْسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ I created the jinn and humankind only to worship Me; * الجنّ name of Sura 72, Medinan sura, so-named because of the reference in verses 1–17 to ‘the Jinn’ 2 hidden beings generally including the jinn and angels, a species of angels said to have been entrusted with guarding the earth and the garden of Paradise (18:50) وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ كَانَ مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَفَسَقَ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِ and We said to the angels, ‘Prostrate yourselves before Adam,’ and they prostrated themselves, except ʾiblīs: he was one of the jinn and so broke away from the command of his Lord.

جِنَّة jinnatun I [n.] madness (7:184) أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا مَا بِصَاحِبِهِمْ مِنْ جِنَّةٍ have they not reflected?, there is no madness in their companion II [collect. n.] 1 jinn (11:119) لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ the word of your Lord will be fulfilled: ‘I shall definitely fill Hell with jinn and men together’ 2 angels (37:158) وَجَعَلُوا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ الْجِنَّةِ نَسَبًا وَلَقَدْ عَلِمَتِ الْجِنَّةُ إِنَّهُمْ لَمُحْضَرُونَ they claim that He has kinship with the angels, yet the angels themselves know that they will be brought before Him [like the rest of His creation].

مَجْنُون majnūn [pass. part.] mad, possessed by a bad spirit (26:27) إِنَّ رَسُولَكُمُ الَّذِي أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ لَمَجْنُونٌ your Messenger who has been sent to you is truly possessed.

جَنَّة jannatun [n., dual جَنَّتَان jannatān, pl. جَنَّات jannāt] 1 garden (17:91) أَوْ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةٌ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَعِنَبٍ or until you have a garden of date palms and vines 2 paradise (7:27) يَا بَنِي آدَمَ لَا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ كَمَا أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُمْ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ children of Adam, do not let Satan seduce you—like his ousting your two parents, from the Garden.

177

177
